name: Beef Tournedos with Gin & Juniper Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 Beef tenderloin steaks (trimmed of fat), 4 ounces each
recipeInstructions:
1. In a measuring cup, combine beef stock, gin, balsamic vinegar, and juniper berries. Set aside.
2. Brush or rub the steaks with vegetable oil. Press coarse black pepper onto the steaks and season with salt.
3. Heat a medium-sized cast-iron or heavy skillet over high heat until nearly smoking.
4. Place the steaks in the heated pan and cook for 2 to 3 minutes per side, or until browned on the outside but still pink inside. Transfer the steaks to a plate and keep warm.
5. Reduce the heat under the skillet to medium. Add the reserved stock mixture to the pan.
6. Cook, stirring to scrape up any brown bits on the bottom of the pan, for 1 to 2 minutes until reduced slightly.
7. Place the steaks on individual plates and spoon the sauce over each. Serves 2. Calories: 242 per serving; Protein: 25 grams; Fat: 10 grams; Carbohydrate: 4 grams; Sodium: 252 milligrams; Cholesterol: 71 milligrams.
